steps.title: Identify the triangle structure
steps.content: The right angle marker (small square) at the bottom right confirms this is a right triangle. The vertical side has length 4, the horizontal side has length b, and the hypotenuse (opposite the right angle) has length 19.

steps.title: Apply the Pythagorean theorem
steps.content:
For any right triangle, the sum of the squares of the two legs equals the square of the hypotenuse:
$$\text{(leg)}_1^2 + \text{(leg)}_2^2 = \text{(hypotenuse)}^2$$
Substituting the side lengths 4, b, and 19:
$$4^2 + b^2 = 19^2$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
